spots.name: Blue Bottle Coffee
spots.description: The iconic third-wave coffee roaster's flagship Japan location. Set in a converted warehouse in the artsy Kiyosumi-Shirakawa district, Blue Bottle brings its minimalist California aesthetic to Tokyo. Known for perfectly executed pour-overs and single-origin espressos. Arrive early on weekends — the line can stretch around the block.

spots.name: Fuglen Coffee
spots.description: A slice of Oslo in Tokyo. This Norwegian import doubles as a coffee shop by day and cocktail bar by night, filled with vintage Scandinavian furniture that's actually for sale. The laid-back Tomigaya neighborhood adds to the charm. Their light-roast filter coffee is some of the best in the city.

spots.name: Onibus Coffee
spots.description: A beloved local roaster tucked away in the trendy Nakameguro area, right by the canal famous for cherry blossoms. The tiny wooden shopfront opens up to a cozy interior where every cup is crafted with care. Their beans are roasted in-house and the baristas are passionate about explaining each origin.

spots.name: Bread, Espresso &
spots.description: Don't let the simple name fool you — this Omotesando gem is a destination for both incredible artisan bread and expertly pulled espresso. The French toast made with their signature Mu bread is legendary. Tucked down a quiet side street away from the Omotesando crowds, it feels like a secret find.

spots.name: LE LABO CAFE KYOTO MACHIYA
spots.description: A tranquil cup of aromatic café latte in a renovated Machiya-style storehouse. Located in a renovated sake brewery over 140 years old, this café welcomes you through a traditional noren curtain and a heavy wooden door, showcasing the unique beams and pillars of the original brewery. The blend of historic architecture and modern coffee culture creates an unforgettable atmosphere.

spots.name: Beatle Momo
spots.description: More than just a café — Beatle Momo is a cozy vinyl bar and restaurant with a welcoming atmosphere and an extensive record collection. A unique destination where music lovers and food enthusiasts come together. Spin through their vinyl collection while enjoying a perfectly brewed coffee or a craft cocktail in the evening.

spots.name: WEEKENDERS COFFEE TOMINOKOJI
spots.description: Nestled in the heart of Kyoto, this hidden gem beckons coffee enthusiasts and casual sippers alike. Located at 560 Honeyanochō, it offers an intimate setting where every cup tells a story. The exceptional brews and minimalist Japanese aesthetic invite you to savor the moment amidst the bustling city. A must-visit for anyone who takes their coffee seriously.

spots.name: % ARABICA Kyoto Higashiyama
spots.description: The original location of the now world-famous % ARABICA chain, perched right along the path to Kiyomizu-dera. The minimal white-and-wood interior frames a stunning view of the Yasaka Pagoda. Come for the Instagram-worthy setting, stay for the genuinely excellent espresso and latte art.
